title: LAMP/RT-LAMP Buffer protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"dshm9","text":"This protocol describes the preparation of reaction buffer for homemade LAMP / RT-LAMP reactions which involves the use of home-brewed BstLF and MMLV enzymes. It also contains the instructions to prepare the enzyme storage buffers.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":43,"length":15},{"style":"bold","offset":215,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"515gv","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cca81","text":"Home-brewed enzymes information:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":31}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3p7kd","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2ggpq","text":"BstLF polymerase:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":6},{"style":"bold","offset":16,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f5097","text":"expression and purification protocol. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1q6c0","text":"The general performance of Homemade buffer seems appropriate and no amplification was obtained for non-template controls (NTC).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ditog","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8lsih","text":"To properly formulate the buffer we tested different KCl concentrations which is known to be a critical factor in the buffer.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c5cnr","text":"For the home-brewed BstLF, we found that 50 mM KCl is the best concentration, displaying equal or better performance than the commercial buffer. No amplification was observed for 150 mM KCl formulation.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6ribu","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"50ed","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"gw9ok","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":null,"data":{}},{"key":"mf5nf","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":4,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"mrvrc","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":null,"data":{}},{"key":"39hr3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"99o31","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5d1vj","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"sdhni","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":5,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"49vl2","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9ao4u","text":"[1] Zhang, Y. et al. steps: DTT (dithiothreitol) To prepare 10 ml of 1 M solution, weigh 1.54 g of DTT powder and dissolve it completely in 80 mL of H2O. Make up the volume to 10 mL with H2O (it can be done in a 15 ml conical tube). Filter-sterilize the solution with a 0.22 μm filter, make 100 uL - 1 ml aliquots and store at -20°C.

EDTA (ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid) To prepare 500 ml of a 500 mM solution: Weigh 93.06 g of EDTA disodium salt dihydrate powder (MW = 372.24 g/mol) and dissolve it in 400 mL of H2O. At this concentration it shouldn't dissolve completely until you up the pH. Adjust to pH 8.0 or 7.1 with concentrated NaOH. It would be needed ~25 uL of 10M NaOH. It should be added slowly in a magnetic stirrer letting the EDTA to dissolve and the pH to equilibrate. Transfer the solution to a graduated cylinder and complete the volume to 500 ml with H2O. Filter-sterilize the solution with a 0.22 μm filter Store at room temperature or 4°C.

Enzyme storage buffer was made based on NEB indicated Enzymes Storage Conditions. DTT should be added as fresh as possible. Add DTT and Glycerol at the moment of assembling the 1X Buffer. Freeze and thaw cycles are not recommended for DTT solutions, always use a fresh aliquot.
To prepare 1.5 mL of 1x Storage Buffer add: 599 µL molecular grade H 2 O 150 µL 10x Storage Buffer 1.5 µL DTT 1M 500 µL Glycerol 100% Mix properly (by vortex), use immediately or store at -20 °C
Glycerol Make an aliquot and sterilize by autoclave.
This (RT-)LAMP assay buffer was made based on NEB 1X Isothermal Amplification Buffer Pack . 10X Isothermal Amplification Buffe r is as follow:
Prepare the following stock solutions: In the " Stocks Solutions " section you can find the preparation of each solution.

Potassium chloride (KCl) To prepare 50 ml of a 1 M solution, weigh 9.3188 g of KCl powder and completely dissolve it in 40 mL of H2O. Make up the volume to 50 mL with H2O (it can be done in a 50 ml conical tube). Filter-sterilize the solution with a 0.22 μm filter and store at room temperature.

Ammonium sulfate ([NH 4 ] 2 SO 4 ) To prepare 50 ml of a 1 M solution, weigh 6.6070 g of (NH4)2SO4 powder and completely dissolve it in 40 mL of H2O. Make up the volume to 50 mL with H2O (it can be done in a 50 ml conical tube). Filter-sterilize the solution with a 0.22 μm filter and store at room temperature.

Magnesium sulfate (MgSO 4 ) To prepare 50 ml of a 1 M solution, weigh 12.3240 g of MgSO4*7H20 powder and completely dissolve it in 40 mL of H2O. Make up the volume to 50 mL with H2O (it can be done in a 50 ml conical tube). Filter-sterilize the solution with a 0.22 μm filter and store at room temperature. It is possible to use MgSO4 instead of heptahidated but it is more hygroscopic and would need desiccating before weighing .

Tris⋅HCl To prepare 250 ml of a 1 M solution: Weigh 30.2850 g of Trizma Base powder and completely dissolve it in 200 mL of H2O. It should be done in a magnetic stirrer. Adjust to pH 8.8 or 7.1 with concentrated HCl (2.5 M). It should be done in a properly calibrated pH-meter, with continuous stirr and giving enough time to let the pH to equilibrate. It is important to check the temperature with a thermometer and use a heater if necessary to reach a temperature solution of 25°C the whole time. Transfer the solution to a graduated cylinder and complete the volume to 250 ml with H2O. Filter-sterilize the solution with a 0.22 μm filter Store at room temperature or 4°C up to six months. Temperature has a significant effect on the pH of Tris buffers. It decreases ~0.028 pH units per each °C increase.

Hydrochloric acid (HCl) To prepare 50 ml of 2.5 M solution: Weight 4.55 g HCl and completely dissolve it in 40 mL of H2O, or add 10.25 mL HCl 37% (d = 1.19) solution to 40 ml H20. Make up the volume to 50 ml with H2O.

Filters: To sterilize the solutions and the assembled buffer 0.22 μm syringe filters of MCE or Cellulose acetate membranes could be used. Specifically we used: BioFil MCE membrane 0.22 μm EDLAB Cellulose acetate 0.22 μm

To prepare 50 mL of 10X Isothermal Amplification Buffer add: 20 mL Molecular H 2 0 10 mL 1M Tris-HCl (pH 8.8 @ 25°C) 5 mL 1M (NH 4 ) 2 SO 4 10 mL 2.5M KCl 1 mL 1M MgSO 4 0.5 mL Tween® 20 Adjust pH 8.8 with NaOH 1 M (~ 560 µL ) Make up the volume to 50 mL with H 2 O (~ 3 mL ) Filter-sterilize the solution with 0.22 μm filter. It shouldn't be autoclaved. The autoclave is not recommended for Tween® 20, (NH 4 ) 2 SO 4 and MgSO 4 solutions. Make aliquots and store at -20 °C . pH variation dynamics (@25 °C) along buffer elaboration.

To prepare 10 mL of 10x Storage Buffer add: 6 mL molecular grade H 2 O 1 mL 1M Tris-HCl ph 7.1 2 mL KCl 2.5M 20 µL EDTA 500 mM 100 µL Triton® X-100 Make up the volume to 10 mL with H 2 O (~ 880 µL ) Make aliquots and store at -20 °C In the " Stocks Solutions " section you can find the preparation of each solution.
creator: Tamara Matute
